heading: Definitions
label: 10284 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act, referred to in par. (14)(C)(i), (D)(i), is , , , which is classified principally to chapter 68 (§ 5121 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 93–28888 Stat. 143section 5121 of Title 42May 22, 1974

CodificationPar. (14)(F) of this section, which was originally enacted as par. (9)(E) by , ceased to have force or effect on . Prior to being omitted, par. (14)(F) included within the definition of public safety officer an individual appointed to the National Disaster Medical System under , The Public Health and Welfare, performing official duties of the Department of Health and Human Services that were related to responding to a public health emergency, potential public health emergency, or other activities for which the National Disaster Medical System was activated and that were determined by the Secretary of Health and Human Services to be hazardous.section 301(d)(2) of Pub. L. 116–22section 300hh–11 of Title 42Oct. 1, 2021Section was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 3796b of Title 42is a corrected version of .Pub. L. 99–591Pub. L. 99–500

children.label: i
children.text: the law enforcement officer or firefighter then was not authorized to act, in the ordinary course, in an official capacity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the member of a rescue squad or ambulance crew then was not authorized or licensed to act, in the ordinary course, by law or by the applicable agency or entity;

children.label: i
children.text: a law enforcement officer or a firefighter to take, who was authorized to act, in the ordinary course, in an official capacity, in the jurisdiction where the action was taken; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a member of a rescue squad or ambulance crew to take, who was authorized or licensed by law and by a pertinent agency or entity to act, in the ordinary course, in the jurisdiction where the action was taken; and

children.label: I
children.text: to prevent, halt, or respond to the immediate consequences of a crime (including an incident of juvenile delinquency); or

children.label: II
children.text: while engaging in a rescue activity or in the provision of emergency medical services;

children.label: I
children.text: while engaging in fire suppression; or

children.label: II
children.text: while engaging in a rescue activity or in the provision of emergency medical services; or

children.label: iii
children.text: by a member of a rescue squad or ambulance crew, while engaging in a rescue activity or in the provision of emergency medical services;

children.label: A
children.text: commissioning of such individual as a law enforcement officer;

children.label: B
children.text: conferral upon such individual of official authority to engage in fire suppression (as an officer or employee of a public fire department or as an officially recognized or designated member of a legally organized volunteer fire department); or

children.label: C
children.text: granting to such individual official authorization or license to engage in a rescue activity, or in the provision of emergency medical services, as a member of a rescue squad, or as a member of an ambulance crew that is (or is a part of) the agency or entity that is sponsoring the individual’s enrollment or admission;

“blind” means an individual who has central visual acuity of 20/200 or less in the better eye with the use of a correcting lens or whose eye is accompanied by a limitation in the fields of vision such that the widest diameter of the visual field subtends an angle no greater than 20 degrees;

children.label: i
children.text: work that involves ordinary or simple tasks, that because of the claimed disability, the claimant cannot perform without significantly more supervision, accommodation, or assistance than is typically provided to an individual without the claimed disability doing similar work;

children.label: ii
children.text: work that involves minimal duties that make few or no demands on the claimant and are of little or no economic value to the employer; or

children.label: iii
children.text: work that is performed primarily for therapeutic purposes and aids the claimant in the physical or mental recovery from the claimed disability; and

children.label: i
children.text: blind;

children.label: ii
children.text: parapalegic; or1So in original. Probably should be “paraplegic;”.1

children.label: iii
children.text: quadriplegic;

“chaplain” includes any individual serving as an officially recognized or designated member of a legally organized volunteer fire department or legally organized police department, or an officially recognized or designated public employee of a legally organized fire or police department who was responding to a fire, rescue, or police emergency;

children.label: A
children.text: 18 years of age or under;

children.label: B
children.text: over 18 years of age and a student as defined in ; orsection 8101 of title 5

children.label: C
children.text: over 18 years of age and incapable of self-support because of physical or mental disability;

“firefighter” includes an individual serving as an officially recognized or designated member of a legally organized volunteer fire department, including an individual who, as such a member, engages in scene security or traffic management as the primary or only duty of the individual during emergency response;

children.label: A
children.text: a post-injury blood alcohol level of .20 per centum or greater; or

children.label: B
children.text: a post-injury blood alcohol level of at least .10 per centum but less than .20 per centum unless the Bureau receives convincing evidence that the public safety officer was not acting in an intoxicated manner immediately prior to his fatal or catastrophic injury;

“law enforcement officer” means an individual involved in crime and juvenile delinquency control or reduction, or enforcement of the criminal laws (including juvenile delinquency), including, but not limited to, police, corrections, probation, parole, and judicial officers;

children.label: A
children.text: is a public agency; or

children.label: i
children.text: is officially authorized or licensed to engage in rescue activity or to provide emergency medical services; and

children.label: ii
children.text: engages in rescue activities or provides emergency medical services as part of an official emergency response system;

“neurocognitive disorder” means a disorder that is characterized by a clinically significant decline in cognitive functioning and may include symptoms and signs such as disturbances in memory, executive functioning (that is, higher-level cognitive processes, such as, regulating attention, planning, inhibiting responses, decision-making), visual-spatial functioning, language, speech, perception, insight, judgment, or an insensitivity to social standards;

children.label: A
children.text: involves lifting articles weighing no more than 10 pounds at a time or occasionally lifting or carrying articles such as docket files, ledgers, or small tools; and

children.label: B
children.text: despite involving sitting on a regular basis, may require walking or standing on an occasional basis;

“public agency” means the United States, any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands of the United States, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any territory or possession of the United States, or any unit of local government, department, agency, or instrumentality of any of the foregoing, and includes (as may be prescribed by regulation hereunder) a legally organized volunteer fire department that is a nonprofit entity and provides services without regard to any particular relationship (such as a subscription) a member of the public may have with such a department; and

children.label: i
children.text: were not unreasonable;

children.label: ii
children.text: would have been within the authority and line of duty of such law enforcement officer or such firefighter to take, had they been taken in a jurisdiction where such law enforcement officer or firefighter was authorized to act, in the ordinary course, in an official capacity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: would have resulted in the payment of full line-of-duty death or disability benefits (as applicable), if any such benefits typically were payable by (or with respect to or on behalf of) such public agency, as of the date the actions were taken;

children.label: B
children.text: a candidate officer who is engaging in an activity or exercise that itself is a formal or required part of the program in which the candidate officer is enrolled or admitted, as provided in this section;

children.label: i
children.text: are related to a major disaster or emergency that has been, or is later, declared to exist with respect to the area under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 5121

children.label: ii
children.text: are determined by the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency to be hazardous duties;

children.label: i
children.text: are related to a major disaster or emergency that has been, or is later, declared to exist with respect to the area under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 5121

children.label: ii
children.text: are determined by the head of the agency to be hazardous duties;

children.label: i
children.text: were not unreasonable;

children.label: ii
children.text: would have been within the authority and line of duty of such member to take, had they been taken in a jurisdiction where such member was authorized or licensed by law and by a pertinent agency or entity to act, in the ordinary course; and

children.label: iii
children.text: would have resulted in the payment of full line-of-duty death or disability benefits (as applicable), if any such benefits typically were payable by (or with respect to or on behalf of) such applicable agency or entity, as of the date the action was taken;

children.label: F
children.text: omitted

children.label: I
children.text: a nuclear materials courier (as defined in ); orsection 8331(27) of title 5

children.label: II
children.text: designated by the Secretary of Energy as a member of an emergency response team; and

children.label: I
children.text: assessing, locating, identifying, securing, rendering safe, or disposing of weapons of mass destruction (as defined in ); orsection 2302 of title 50

children.label: II
children.text: managing the immediate consequences of a radiological release or exposure.
